params.name: Helmholtz Coil
params.description:
On May 10, 1872 AD, the Helmholtz coil was designed.
The Helmholtz Coil is a device that produces a uniform magnetic field in a small area, named after its inventor, the German physicist Helmholtz. The device consists of a large number of identical circular conductor coils, each carrying a current in the same direction to create a magnetic field with minimal inhomogeneity. Due to its open nature, it is convenient for the insertion and removal of other instruments, and it is also beneficial fo …
